page: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. The processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance is equipped with 976 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.25 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core performance. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU evaluations. Its cryptographic capabilities are highlighted by strong performance in Passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction tests. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, with read-only large block transfers reaching 151.0 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 61.57 GB/sec. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 25%, while single-core performance remains in the average tier.

Given its high memory density and dedicated compute resources, the m3-megamem-64 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring massive memory footprints relative to compute capacity. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, heavy data compilation tasks, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (32 physical cores) running at 2.6 GHz and 976 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 15.25 GB, making it suitable for memory-intensive applications. The server features a 54 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and AES instructions, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, high database operations throughput, and efficient static web serving, while single-core metrics and memory bandwidth remain average. The qualitative cost profile reflects its high resource density, making it an efficient choice for large-scale in-memory databases, software compilation, and enterprise workloads that demand substantial memory capacity without requiring local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 976 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring 976 GB of RAM and 64 dedicated vCPUs for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 15.25 GB per core
Strong multi-core CPU and database operations performance
